reasoning: The 360-day average of riots prior to March 15, 2026 is 6.833 per month (approx 82 total riots). This period includes a localized but massive spike of protests and riots in late September and October 2025, known as the "Gen Z 212" protests, described as the largest unrest in Morocco since 2011. Because this intense, multi-week span of nationwide rioting is included in the 360-day average, the average is mathematically inflated compared to a typical month. Since those specific protests ended in October and were met with a severe crackdown, recent news (early 2026) reports no ongoing comparable unrest. The baseline rate of riots outside of that spike is likely much lower (closer to 2-4 per month). Under a Poisson distribution with a mean of ~3.5, the probability of seeing 7 or more riots in a given 30-day period is only around 10%. Without any present indications of unrest returning in February/March 2026, the probability that the next 30-day window will exceed the inflated 360-day average is very low.
